featureName: BUG-042-005: Compose contract test missing adversarial coverage for literal-bind / default-fallback bind on prometheus

executionHistory.evidence: Inspected internal/deploy/compose_contract_test.go and observed: (1) TestComposeContract_AdversarialOllamaLiteralBind covers ollama literal + default-fallback (BUG-042-003); (2) TestComposeContract_AdversarialDefaultFallbackBind covers smackerel-core default-fallback + smackerel-ml default-fallback + smackerel-ml literal (BUG-042-004); (3) NO adversarial coverage exists for the literal 127.0.0.1: form OR the forbidden default-fallback ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:-127.0.0.1}: form on prometheus. The runtime behavior of assertComposeContract is correct (it rejects both forms today via strict strings.HasPrefix against requiredPrometheusPrefix), but the test surface has a coverage gap that would let a future maintainer accidentally relax the prefix check (e.g., to a too-loose strings.Contains substring match) without any test failing RED — the live-file test would catch a regression in the live file itself, but only this synthetic adversarial test catches a relaxation of the assertion. Direct HL-RESCAN-010 finding from the home-lab readiness re-scan against da263ffe.

Modified internal/deploy/compose_contract_test.go (only file changed): appended new persistent in-tree test function TestComposeContract_AdversarialPrometheusLiteralBindAndFallbackForms at end of file (after TestComposeContract_AdversarialDefaultFallbackBind). Function uses a table-driven sweep with two sub-cases: (1) prometheus uses literal 127.0.0.1: bind (spec 020 form); (2) prometheus uses ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:-127.0.0.1}: default-fallback bind. Each sub-case builds a compose YAML fixture using fmt.Sprintf, calls assertComposeContract, asserts non-nil error, asserts error mentions 'prometheus', and asserts error mentions at least one of the anchor terms [spec 049, spec 042, fail-loud, ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:?, ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:-127.0.0.1}, literal 127.0.0.1:] via a Contains loop. HL-RESCAN-010 attribution in docstring (lines 697 + 725) + failure-case t.Fatalf message (line 762) + anchor-list assertion failure message (line 782). Total diff: 97 insertions / 0 deletions. NO change to assertComposeContract function body, NO change to requiredCorePrefix/requiredMLPrefix/requiredPrometheusPrefix/requiredOllamaPrefix constants, NO change to deploy/compose.deploy.yml.

Targeted contract suite GREEN: go test -count=1 -v -run '^TestComposeContract|^TestVulnGateContract|^TestBundleHashContract' ./internal/deploy/... PASS in 0.019s — all 9 top-level TestComposeContract_* tests + all sub-tests + TestVulnGateContract_LiveFile + 10 VulnGate adversarial sub-tests + TestBundleHashContract_LiveFile + 4 BundleHash adversarial sub-tests. Cross-package smoke: ./smackerel.sh test unit --go returns [go-unit] go test ./... finished OK — every internal/* and cmd/* package returns ok. New tests run as part of the persistent in-tree adversarial suite — every ./smackerel.sh test unit --go invocation (CI + developer pre-push) re-validates the contract.

No simplification opportunities surfaced. The fix is the minimum viable change: 1 file modified, 97 insertions / 0 deletions. The table-driven sub-case pattern is reused from TestComposeContract_AdversarialOllamaLiteralBind (2 sub-cases) and TestComposeContract_AdversarialDefaultFallbackBind (3 sub-cases) — established convention in this file. Sub-case count is exactly two (no duplicate of network_mode which is already covered by TestComposeContract_AdversarialNetworkModeHostBypass for prometheus among other services). Anchor-list assertion uses Contains loop over six terms (not a single exact-string match) to stay flexible to future cosmetic error-message wording changes.

No flaky-test or stability concerns surfaced. The new tests are deterministic: no time.Now, no random IDs, no goroutine concurrency, no I/O beyond static YAML parse on small fixture strings. Both sub-tests PASS in 0.014s with zero variance across re-runs.

Security review: A05 Security Misconfiguration — the fix STRENGTHENS the existing spec 042 / spec 049 / Gate G028 SST policy by mechanically locking prometheus compliance against future drift. No new attack surface introduced. No PII handled (the contract test operates on YAML structure only). The fix is the smackerel-side primitive that prevents accidental publication of an insecure compose file (literal-bind to 0.0.0.0 via the spec 020 form, or silent fallback to 127.0.0.1 via the forbidden default-fallback form) for the prometheus monitoring service — closing the last operator-facing service coverage gap in the four-service matrix (smackerel-core, smackerel-ml, ollama, prometheus).

Static checks: gofmt -l internal/deploy/ empty (exit 0); go vet ./internal/deploy/... exit 0. Cross-package smoke: ./smackerel.sh test unit --go returns [go-unit] go test ./... finished OK — every internal/* and cmd/* package PASS. RED→GREEN proof: temporarily replaced the prometheus branch's strings.HasPrefix(p, requiredPrometheusPrefix) with a too-loose strings.Contains(p, "${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:") substring check via replace_string_in_file (the most realistic accidental relaxation a maintainer might introduce — would silently accept the forbidden default-fallback form because it contains the substring, but would still reject the literal 127.0.0.1: form because the literal does not contain ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:), kept the new tests in place, re-ran. Observed: prometheus default-fallback sub-case FAILS with the expected `the contract is tautological — it would NOT catch a regression to the spec 020 literal form or to the default-fallback ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:-127.0.0.1} form for prometheus; HL-RESCAN-010 prometheus literal-bind / default-fallback coverage gap is reintroduced` message; the literal-bind sub-case PASSes unchanged because the relaxation specifically smuggles only the default-fallback form (positive cross-check that the new test correctly attributes failures to the specific regression vector). Restored the strict HasPrefix check via replace_string_in_file → both sub-cases PASS GREEN. Existing TestComposeContract_LiveFile + 9 pre-existing TestComposeContract_* adversarial tests + TestVulnGateContract_LiveFile + 10 sub-tests + TestBundleHashContract_LiveFile + 4 sub-tests all PASS unchanged. Full evidence in report.md > Test Evidence + > Audit Evidence.

OWASP A05 review GREEN (improved). Privacy review GREEN (no PII). Minimum-viable-change audit GREEN: 1 file modified (internal/deploy/compose_contract_test.go only, +97 / -0 lines). No production runtime Go/Python code modified. No assertComposeContract function body edited. No constant declaration edited. No deploy/compose.deploy.yml change. No docker-compose.yml change. No config/smackerel.yaml change. No Dockerfile change. No CI workflow change. No script change. Cross-package smoke clean. All canaries (9 pre-existing TestComposeContract_* + LiveFile + VulnGate 11 + BundleHash 5) PASS unchanged. Generic-only constraint preserved (no real hostnames/IPs/tailnet identifiers; uses SST substitution forms exclusively). Promotion decision: SHIP_IT.

Bug spec/design/scopes/scenario-manifest/report/uservalidation/state artifacts authored at specs/042-tailnet-edge-bind-pattern/bugs/BUG-042-005-prometheus-default-fallback-bind-adversarial-coverage/. No external doc updates required: the fix is purely a contract-test extension; no operator workflow change, no Deployment.md change, no copilot-instructions.md change, no README change. The bug-local scenario-manifest.json declares 2 scenarios (SCN-042-005-A, SCN-042-005-B). Foreign-owned spec 042 + spec 049 content untouched per bubbles.devops mode artifact-ownership rules.

certification.auditNote: Home-lab readiness re-scan (system review session 2026-05-14, against HEAD da263ffe) finding HL-RESCAN-010 (P3). The compose contract test internal/deploy/compose_contract_test.go enforced the spec 042 / spec 049 / Gate G028 fail-loud SST contract correctly at runtime via assertComposeContract, but the test surface had a coverage gap: NO adversarial sub-case proved rejection of the literal 127.0.0.1: spec 020 form OR the forbidden default-fallback ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:-127.0.0.1}: bind form on prometheus (the live-file test would catch a regression in the live file itself, but only synthetic adversarial tests catch a relaxation of the assertion code). A future maintainer who accidentally relaxes the strict strings.HasPrefix prefix check (e.g., to a too-loose strings.Contains substring match) on the prometheus branch would slip past every existing test. Fix adds new persistent in-tree TestComposeContract_AdversarialPrometheusLiteralBindAndFallbackForms function with 2 table-driven sub-cases covering both regression vectors, anchor-list assertion over [spec 049, spec 042, fail-loud, ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:?, ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:-127.0.0.1}, literal 127.0.0.1:], HL-RESCAN-010 attribution in docstring + failure-case messages. RED→GREEN proven via temporary substring-softening of the prometheus branch in assertComposeContract: prometheus default-fallback sub-case FAILS with the expected the contract is tautological message; the literal-bind sub-case continues to PASS unchanged because the literal does not contain the ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS: substring (positive cross-check that the new test correctly attributes failures to the specific regression vector). Restoration via replace_string_in_file returns the suite to all-PASS GREEN. Cross-package smoke (./smackerel.sh test unit --go) PASS — every internal/* and cmd/* package returns ok. Spec 042 BUG-042-001 + BUG-042-002 + BUG-042-003 + BUG-042-004 canaries + spec 047 vuln-gate canary + BUG-047-001 bundle-hash canary all PASS unchanged. Generic-only constraint preserved: zero real hostnames/IPs/tailnet identifiers introduced. Single-file code change: only internal/deploy/compose_contract_test.go (+97 / -0 lines). Bug-packet artifacts contained in specs/042-tailnet-edge-bind-pattern/bugs/BUG-042-005-prometheus-default-fallback-bind-adversarial-coverage/. Foreign-owned spec 042 + spec 049 content (spec.md, design.md, scopes.md, state.json, uservalidation.md, parent report.md) untouched per bubbles.devops mode artifact-ownership rules. Promotion decision: SHIP_IT.

scopeProgress.name: Add adversarial coverage for prometheus literal-bind and default-fallback regressions
scopeProgress.notes: 1 file modified (internal/deploy/compose_contract_test.go, +97 / -0 lines). New persistent in-tree TestComposeContract_AdversarialPrometheusLiteralBindAndFallbackForms function with 2 table-driven sub-cases: (a) prometheus literal 127.0.0.1: bind (spec 020 form), (b) prometheus ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:-127.0.0.1}: default-fallback bind. Each sub-case asserts non-nil error AND error mentions 'prometheus' AND error mentions at least one of [spec 049, spec 042, fail-loud, ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:?, ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS:-127.0.0.1}, literal 127.0.0.1:]. HL-RESCAN-010 attribution in docstring + failure-case t.Fatalf + anchor-list assertion failure. Both sub-cases PASS GREEN in 0.014s. RED proof: 1 expected FAIL on prometheus default-fallback sub-case when prometheus branch is substring-softened; 1 PASS on prometheus literal-bind sub-case (positive cross-check — literal does not contain ${HOST_BIND_ADDRESS: substring). GREEN proof: restoration returns both PASS. Targeted contract suite + cross-package smoke clean. Full DoD evidence inline in scopes.md.
